sessions.security_1: • Revoke unfamiliar sessions immediately and change your password.

notifications.title: Notifications
notifications.event_ticket_created_desc: When a new ticket is submitted
notifications.event_status_changed_desc: When an assigned ticket status changes
notifications.event_comment_added_desc: When a comment is added to an assigned ticket
notifications.event_assigned_desc: When a ticket is assigned to me
notifications.pref_desc: Manage notification preferences and watched tickets.
notifications.no_watches_hint: Use the "🔕 Watch this ticket" button in the ticket detail sidebar.
notifications.watches_hint: You will receive email notifications for status changes and comments on watched tickets.

portal.title: IT Support Request
portal.no_gitlab: You can submit an IT support request without a GitLab account.
portal.content_placeholder: Please describe the issue, when it started, and its impact
portal.success_hint: You can track progress via the link sent to your email or the link below.

shortcuts.title: Keyboard Shortcuts
shortcuts.hint: Shortcuts are disabled when an input field is focused.

gantt.legend_dep_title: Dependency arrow (A → B: A affects B)
gantt.link_blocks_desc: A must complete before B can start
gantt.link_duplicate_desc: Duplicate ticket covering the same issue

reports.title: Reports
reports.subtitle: Ticket status · satisfaction · agent performance by period
reports.trend_no_data: No snapshot data for this period.
reports.trend_snapshot_hint: Snapshots are generated automatically at midnight each day.

ticket_new.desc: Please describe your issue in detail and we'll resolve it quickly.
ticket_new.content_placeholder:
When did it start, what are the symptoms,
and what have you already tried?

users.title: User Management
users.search_placeholder: Search by name, username, email, org...

sla.title: SLA Policies
sla.description: Set SLA target times by priority. Changes apply to newly created tickets.
sla.priority_high_desc: Major feature failure · Rapid resolution needed

service_types.title: Service Types
service_types.description: Service types are used as ticket and KB categories. Internal keys are generated automatically.
service_types.options_desc: Detail options shown after selecting a service type when creating a ticket.
service_types.options_empty_hint: Adding options will show quick-select buttons when creating tickets.
service_types.delete_confirm:
Delete "{emoji} {label}" service type?
Deletion will be rejected if tickets are using it.
service_types.delete_title_disabled: Cannot delete: {count} tickets are using this type. Use disable instead.

assignment_rules.title: Assignment Rules
assignment_rules.description: Automatically assign agents to tickets when conditions match. Higher priority numbers are applied first.
assignment_rules.conditions_title: Conditions (leave empty to apply to all tickets)

automation_rules.title: Automation Rules
automation_rules.description: Define actions to run automatically when events occur.
automation_rules.conditions_empty: No conditions — applies to all events
automation_rules.actions_empty: Please add at least one action

templates.title: Ticket Templates
templates.description: Manage description templates that auto-fill when creating tickets.

quick_replies.title: Quick Reply Templates
quick_replies.description: Register frequently used replies to quickly select them when entering comments.

announcements.title: Announcements / Banner Management
announcements.description: Active announcements are displayed as banners at the top of the screen for all logged-in users.
announcements.field_title_placeholder: Title displayed in bold on the banner
announcements.field_content_placeholder: Supporting text shown next to the title in gray
announcements.preview_empty: Enter a title to see the preview
announcements.guide_1: Active announcements are displayed immediately at the top of the screen for all logged-in users.
announcements.guide_2: Even if users close it with the × button, it reappears on page refresh (session maintained).
announcements.guide_3: Setting an expiry date/time will automatically hide the banner after that time.
announcements.guide_4: Click the status toggle (● Visible / ○ Hidden) to instantly activate/deactivate.
announcements.guide_5: Use Critical (🚨) for server outages and security notices, Warning (⚠️) for maintenance, Info (ℹ️) for policy changes.
announcements.guide_6: Multiple active announcements are stacked together. It is recommended to only activate urgent ones.

notification_channels.title: Notification Channel Settings
notification_channels.description: Toggle email and Telegram sending at runtime. Infrastructure environment variables (SMTP / Bot Token, etc.) must be configured separately.
notification_channels.email_description: Sends emails to relevant users when events occur such as ticket creation, status changes, comments, and SLA warnings.
notification_channels.telegram_description: Sends key events such as ticket creation and SLA violations to the IT team channel via Telegram messages.
notification_channels.slack_description: Sends ticket creation, status change, and SLA violation notifications to the IT channel via Slack Incoming Webhook. The send_slack action in automation rules also uses this channel.
notification_channels.unconfigured_hint: Actual sending is disabled because environment variables are not set. Enabling will not send notifications.
notification_channels.how_1: If environment variables (NOTIFICATION_ENABLED / TELEGRAM_ENABLED / SLACK_ENABLED) are disabled, notifications will not be sent regardless of this setting.
notification_channels.how_2: If environment variables are enabled, this toggle controls sending at runtime.
notification_channels.how_3: Changes take effect immediately and propagate to all servers within 60 seconds.

role_labels.title: Role Display Names
role_labels.subtitle: Customize the display names of user roles. Changes are reflected across the UI immediately.
role_labels.note: Role codes (admin, agent, etc.) are not changed. Only display names are updated.
role_labels.desc_admin: All admin features, system settings, user role changes
role_labels.desc_agent: Ticket handling, SLA management, internal reports
role_labels.desc_pl: View/edit all tickets, KB authoring, reassignment, bulk actions
role_labels.desc_developer: Handle own tickets, view KB, trigger GitLab pipelines
role_labels.desc_user: Create/view own tickets, read KB, request service catalog

search_index.title: Full-text Search Index
search_index.subtitle: PostgreSQL pg_trgm GIN index status and manual sync
search_index.no_gin: No GIN indexes found. Verify migrations (0053, 0056) are applied.
search_index.hint_webhook: Issues are indexed in real time on GitLab webhook events.
search_index.hint_beat: Celery Beat runs a full sync every 30 minutes.
search_index.hint_fallback: Search falls back from index to GitLab API on miss.
search_index.hint_manual: Use manual sync when the index is empty or suspected out of sync.
search_index.manual_sync_desc: Indexes all GitLab issues immediately. Celery must be running.

email_ingest.title: Email Ingest Monitoring
email_ingest.subtitle: IMAP-based email ingest configuration and Celery task status
email_ingest.manual_run_desc: Process the inbox immediately without waiting for the schedule.
email_ingest.disabled_hint: Set IMAP_ENABLED=true and IMAP_HOST, IMAP_USER, IMAP_PASSWORD environment variables.

failed_notifications.title: Failed Notifications
failed_notifications.subtitle: Records of notifications that exceeded the max retry count.

api_keys.title: API Keys
api_keys.subtitle: Manage API keys for external systems to access the ITSM API.
api_keys.key_shown_once: API key created — shown only once!
api_keys.key_save_hint: This key cannot be retrieved again. Store it securely immediately.
api_keys.empty_hint: Use for CI/CD pipelines or external system integration.
api_keys.revoke_confirm: Revoke API key "{name}"? This action cannot be undone.

monitoring.title: System Monitoring
monitoring.subtitle: API server, Celery, Redis status and external monitoring tool links.
monitoring.flush_confirm: Flush ITSM cache? Responses may be temporarily slower.
monitoring.tool_flower_desc: Real-time Celery task and worker monitoring
monitoring.external_note: * Requires the Docker Compose services to be running. Accessed via nginx proxy at /flower, /grafana, /prometheus.

nav.default_subtitle: Manage users, SLA, automation and security settings
nav.no_permission_hint: Contact an administrator to request access.

outbound_webhooks.title: Outbound Webhooks
outbound_webhooks.subtitle: Forward ITSM events to Slack, Teams, or external systems.
outbound_webhooks.empty_hint: Integrate with Slack or Teams for real-time notifications.

email_templates.title: Email Templates
email_templates.subtitle: Customize event-based email notifications. Jinja2 syntax supported.
email_templates.subject: Subject
email_templates.enable_template: Enable template (uses hardcoded default when disabled)
email_templates.select_hint: Select a template on the left to edit.
email_templates.ev_ticket_created_desc: Sent to IT team and assignee on new ticket
email_templates.ev_status_changed_desc: Sent to requester and subscribers on status change
email_templates.ev_comment_added_desc: Sent to requester and subscribers on new comment
email_templates.ev_sla_warning_desc: Sent to IT team 60 minutes before SLA deadline
email_templates.ev_sla_breach_desc: Sent to IT team when SLA is exceeded

labels.title: GitLab Label Sync
labels.subtitle: Verify and sync all ITSM labels with GitLab projects and groups.
labels.structure_1: Workflow/SLA constants. This page only shows status.
labels.structure_3: Sync principle — labels are only created/updated; never deleted (deletion auto-removes from issues).
labels.structure_4: Project + Group — both get labels so forwarded projects share the same set.

celery.title: Celery Monitoring
celery.subtitle: Real-time worker/queue/task status via Flower API. Auto-refresh every 30s.
celery.flower_hint: Ensure the Flower container (itsm-flower-1) is running.

custom_fields.delete_confirm: Deleting '{label}' will also remove its values from all tickets. Continue?
custom_fields.title: Custom Fields
custom_fields.subtitle: Define custom fields to add to tickets.

recurring.title: Recurring Tickets
recurring.subtitle: Manage tickets that are automatically generated on a schedule.
recurring.cron_hint: min hour day month weekday — e.g., 0 9 1 * * (1st of month 09:00)
recurring.empty_hint: Create your first one with the '+ Add recurring ticket' button.

ip_allowlist.title: IP Access Restriction
ip_allowlist.subtitle_pre: Manage IP ranges allowed to access admin APIs (
ip_allowlist.subtitle_post: ). If empty, all IPs are allowed.
ip_allowlist.my_ip_blocked_title: ⛔ Current IP ({ip}) is not in the allowlist
ip_allowlist.my_ip_blocked_desc: Saving will terminate your session and block future access. Add your IP first.
ip_allowlist.empty_hint: If empty, admin API is accessible from all IPs.
ip_allowlist.warn_1_suffix: is included in the allowlist first.
ip_allowlist.warn_4_prefix: Behind a reverse proxy like Nginx, the

db_cleanup.title: DB Cleanup Automation
db_cleanup.subtitle: Selectively clean old logs, notifications, KB versions and optimize the DB.
db_cleanup.task_audit_desc: Delete audit logs older than {days} days.
db_cleanup.task_audit_confirm: Permanently delete {n} audit logs older than {days} days.
db_cleanup.task_notif_desc: Delete read notifications older than {days} days.
db_cleanup.task_notif_confirm: Permanently delete {n} read notifications older than {days} days.
db_cleanup.task_kb_desc: Keep only the latest {n} versions per KB article.
db_cleanup.task_kb_confirm: Permanently delete {count} old versions (beyond the latest {n} per article).
db_cleanup.vacuum_desc: Refresh table statistics and reclaim unused pages. Best run after cleanup.
db_cleanup.vacuum_confirm_desc: Runs PostgreSQL VACUUM ANALYZE. May take several seconds to minutes.

business_hours.title: SLA Business Hours
business_hours.subtitle: SLA deadlines are calculated in business hours. E.g., a 4-hour SLA policy received at 5 PM becomes due at 10 AM next day.
business_hours.weekly_hint: 💡 Inactive days are excluded from SLA calculations. If no hours are set, 24/7 is assumed.
business_hours.holidays_desc: Holidays are automatically excluded from business hours.

workload.title: User Workload & Performance
workload.subtitle: Evaluate performance based on assigned tickets, completion rate, SLA, and customer satisfaction.

service_catalog.title: Service Catalog
service_catalog.subtitle: Manage items available to request from the portal.

escalation.gitlab_auto_hint: GitLab account info will be filled automatically. You can change above.
escalation.user_select_hint: Selecting a user auto-fills GitLab info.
escalation.title: SLA Escalation Policies
escalation.subtitle: Define actions to execute automatically on SLA breach/warning.
escalation.policy_name_placeholder: e.g., [Critical] Notify admin immediately on SLA breach

ai_settings.title: AI Settings
ai_settings.subtitle: Use OpenAI or Ollama to auto-classify, summarize tickets, and suggest KB articles.
ai_settings.enable_ai_hint: When disabled, all AI features are paused.
ai_settings.provider_hint: 💡 In air-gapped environments, only Ollama is available. OpenAI requires external internet.
ai_settings.api_key_change_placeholder: Enter new key to change (blank keeps current)
ai_settings.model_name_hint: Select from list or enter manually
ai_settings.model_examples_prefix: e.g., llama3.2, mistral, qwen2.5 — download via
ai_settings.feature_summarize_desc: AI summarizes comment thread in ticket detail
ai_settings.test_tooltip: Can test with current form settings even before saving
ai_settings.info_1: • OpenAI API keys are encrypted in DB and never shown.
ai_settings.info_2_prefix: • For Ollama, start the service first with
ai_settings.info_3: • Connection test uses the saved (enabled) settings.

faq.answer_hint: (line break: Enter, bullet: • prefix, number: ①②③④ prefix)
faq.empty_hint: Import legacy FAQ or add a new item.

onboarding.step_admin_desc: Manage users, automation rules, notification channels, and system settings.
